title: Authorization Middleware Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of middleware in securing modern Express.js applications.

questions.question: What is the primary role of Authorization Middleware in Express.js?
options: To verify that a user has the correct permission before processing a request.
To automatically generate documentation for each route.

questions.question: Why is it important to run Authentication middleware before Authorization middleware?
options: Because authentication is faster than authorization.
Because you must first know *who* the user is before you can determine *what* they are allowed to do.
It's not necessary; you can run them in any order.

questions.question: What does a typically-structured RBAC authorization middleware do?
options: It checks if the user has one of the allowed roles for a specific route.
It automatically redirects the user to the login page.

questions.question: Which HTTP status code is typically returned if a user is authenticated but doesn't have the required permissions?
What does 'DRY' stand for in the context of writing middleware?
